title: betula rugosa
intro: Betula rugosa, also known as the paper birch or river birch, is a deciduous tree native to eastern North America. The bark of this tree has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Betula rugosa include reducing fever, relieving pain and swelling, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Betula rugosa can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and salves to utilize its medicinal properties.
